bonuses.leadin: Answer some questions about the work of Claude Monet, for 10 points each.
parts: A Monet painting of a Sunrise gave its name to this artistic movement, which used visible brush strokes to accurately depict different lighting conditions.
The sky in several of Monet’s paintings of the Houses of Parliament is dominated by this color. This is the color of the sun in Impression, Sunrise.
Late in his life, Monet made a single trip to Venice, and painted a rainbow-coloured depiction of this church at dusk. This monastery church houses Tintoretto’s Last Supper.
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: Answer some questions about the work of Claude Monet, for 10 points each.
parts_sanitized: A Monet painting of a Sunrise gave its name to this artistic movement, which used visible brush strokes to accurately depict different lighting conditions.
The sky in several of Monet's paintings of the Houses of Parliament is dominated by this color. This is the color of the sun in Impression, Sunrise.
Late in his life, Monet made a single trip to Venice, and painted a rainbow-coloured depiction of this church at dusk. This monastery church houses Tintoretto's Last Supper.

bonuses.leadin: The façade of this building was completed by Vincenzo Scamozzi, working on a design from another architect. For 10 points each:
parts: Identify this building, whose white, temple-like façade was designed to be seen from the city across a gap of water. Tintoretto's diagonal Last Supper is located in this building.

This architect of the Villa Rotonda died before his work on San Giorgio Maggiore was complete. Thomas Jefferson heavily plagiarized this author of the Four Books of Architecture in buildings such as Monticello.

This Canadian-American architect wrote a monograph about Palladio titled The Perfect House. This onetime architecture critic for Slate also wrote Home: A Short History of an Idea and a microhistory of the screwdriver titled One Good Turn.
answers: Church of San Giorgio Maggiore [or Basilica of San Giorgio Maggiore]
answers_sanitized: Church of San Giorgio Maggiore [or Basilica of San Giorgio Maggiore]
Andrea Palladio [or Andrea Di Pietro della Gondola]
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: The facade of this building was completed by Vincenzo Scamozzi, working on a design from another architect. For 10 points each:
parts_sanitized: Identify this building, whose white, temple-like facade was designed to be seen from the city across a gap of water. Tintoretto's diagonal Last Supper is located in this building.

This architect of the Villa Rotonda died before his work on San Giorgio Maggiore was complete. Thomas Jefferson heavily plagiarized this author of the Four Books of Architecture in buildings such as Monticello.

This Canadian-American architect wrote a monograph about Palladio titled The Perfect House. This onetime architecture critic for Slate also wrote Home: A Short History of an Idea and a microhistory of the screwdriver titled One Good Turn.

bonuses.leadin: This building, which is sometimes named for the Capra brothers, was completed by Vincenzo Scamozzi. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this Andrea Palladio masterpiece best known for its four projecting porticos.
The Villa Rotonda was an important influence on this Charlottesville, Virginia estate, which was designed by Thomas Jefferson.
Palladio designed this church which sits on a namesake Venice island and has a bright white façade. Tintoretto’s The Last Supper is housed in this church.
answers_sanitized: Villa Rotonda [or: La Rotonda, Villa la Rotonda]
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: This building, which is sometimes named for the Capra brothers, was completed by Vincenzo Scamozzi. For 10 points each:
parts_sanitized: Name this Andrea Palladio masterpiece best known for its four projecting porticos.

Palladio designed this church which sits on a namesake Venice island and has a bright white facade. Tintoretto's The Last Supper is housed in this church.
